rationale: Review 1's fiberHomeomorph_symm finding is the decisive winner for api-design: the diff exposes a MonoidHom into ≃ₜ (from a Group source) but only documents mul/one interactions, so omitting the inverse compatibilty (and its canonical statement) leaves an incomplete exposed representation that any maintainer would want completed. Both reviews correctly target the same two .mp/.mpr stabilizer lemmas as redundant bloat; Review 2's @[simp] request is a minor attribute preference whose absence is not an api flaw relative to the diff.
